authoritative_sources.title: Shortening the Securities Transaction Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC final rule is the primary public rule source for T+1 settlement and Rule 15c6-2 policies and procedures governing same-day allocation, confirmation, and affirmation expectations.
used_for: same-day allocation, confirmation, and affirmation expectations
written-assurance and coordination posture with advisers and custodians
policy and procedure requirements for institutional post-trade readiness
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains when middle office may treat trade instructions as ready for downstream settlement and requires explicit evidence of allocation and affirmation dependencies.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Alert: Division of Examinations T+1 Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC examinations guidance is authoritative for the evidence exam staff expects around trade allocations, affirmations, books and records, testing, and process changes supporting T+1 readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Readiness evidence review and exception escalation
used_for: control design around middle-office process changes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to documented evidence and blocks unsupported readiness claims when testing, records, or operational coordination artifacts are absent.

authoritative_sources.title: Re-Imagining Post-Trade
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC is the core U.S. post-trade market infrastructure operator and this workflow reference is authoritative for CTM-based allocation, central matching, affirmation, and trade-manager process semantics.
used_for: distinction between manual and centralized post-trade processing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic middle-office workflow stages, output language, and evidence expectations around central matching and affirmation readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: ALERT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC's ALERT reference is authoritative for how institutional participants manage, validate, and distribute standing settlement instructions in post-trade workflows.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains middle-office setup checks so SSI mismatches trigger exception handling instead of unsupported defaulting to one source.

authoritative_sources.title: T+1 Securities Settlement Industry Implementation Playbook
authoritative_sources.publisher: Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association and Investment Company Institute
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SIFMA and ICI playbook is a recognized industry implementation reference that captures buy-side, sell-side, prime-broker, documentation, testing, and funding dependencies affected by T+1 transition work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Prime-broker and cross-team readiness dependency review
used_for: cross-functional dependency mapping for post-trade readiness
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds realistic middle-office dependency and tenant-adaptation scenarios while remaining subordinate to SEC and DTCC sources.

authoritative_sources.title: Introducing the Legal Entity Identifier (LEI)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GLEIF operates the global LEI system and is the authoritative public source for legal-entity identity reference data used to validate funds, counterparties, and market participants.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to named entity records and avoids readiness claims when counterparty or fund identity evidence is incomplete.

authoritative_sources.title: Principles for Financial Market Infrastructures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPMI-IOSCO PFMI is the recognized public control standard for settlement risk, operational resilience, and post-trade control expectations across financial market infrastructures.
used_for: boundary between readiness review and final settlement ownership
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to recommendation and routing behavior when settlement-control or operational-risk issues exceed middle-office authority.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate trade identifiers, account masters, LEIs, mandate restrictions, counterparty eligibility, and required tenant context before middle-office work may proceed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Settlement-instruction and account-access readiness
workflow_stages.description: Confirm custodian setup, prime-broker or give-up mapping, sub-account readiness, and standing settlement instructions across the governing systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation, matching, and affirmation readiness
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether allocation enrichment, CTM or matching workflow status, and affirmation timing are complete enough for downstream settlement and servicing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and adjacent-lane routing
workflow_stages.description: Hold, rework, or route cases when SSIs conflict, prime-broker documentation is missing, mandates block the request, or the work belongs to onboarding, settlements, collateral, or corporate actions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and downstream handoff
workflow_stages.description: Produce audit-ready readiness or routing records only when the underlying trade, account, SSI, and affirmation evidence supports closure of the middle-office task.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: OMS or EMS fills, block orders, allocations, and matched-trade records
Fund, client, and counterparty account masters with LEIs, sub-account identifiers, and authorized-party records
Investment-guideline checks, restricted-list outputs, counterparty-eligibility records, and approval histories
Standing settlement instructions, custodian account setup records, DVP or RVP elections, and prime-broker or give-up agreements
CTM, matching, confirmation, and affirmation statuses with timestamps
Exception tickets, reconciliation notes, case-management records, and downstream handoff evidence
systems_of_record: OMS or EMS trade blotter and allocation systems
Investment book of record or post-trade accounting system
Client, fund, and counterparty reference-data master
Custodian account master and settlement instruction repository
Central matching or affirmation platforms such as CTM-connected workflow systems
Document management, approval workflow, and case-management systems
Compliance rules, restricted-list, and mandate-control systems

allowed: Determine whether available trade, account, SSI, and authorization evidence supports readiness, hold, rework, routing, or completion within the middle-office lane.
Produce readiness summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to named post-trade artifacts.
Identify when onboarding, compliance, settlements, collateral, or corporate-action specialists must own the next step.
escalate: SSI, custodian, or affirmation records conflict materially across sources.
A mandate, restricted-list, legal-documentation, or jurisdiction issue changes whether the trade may proceed.
The request requires settlement release, account-opening, or final compliance authority.
refuse: The request asks for front-office discretion, legal interpretation, KYC completion, mandate override, or settlement approval outside the lane.
The request asks to mark work complete without authoritative readiness evidence.
Required tenant facts, account setup, entity identity, or systems-of-record context are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing downstream work without verified account, LEI, mandate, or counterparty eligibility evidence.
Treating internal trackers, email, or CRM notes as equivalent to CTM, SSI, account-master, or custodian systems of record.
Selecting one SSI source when ALERT, tenant records, and custodian instructions conflict materially.
Ignoring same-day allocation or affirmation gaps and labeling a trade ready for settlement under T+1 expectations.
Letting urgency override restricted-list, mandate, or approval controls that are explicit prerequisites for the lane.
Collapsing middle office into generic onboarding, settlements, corporate actions, or collateral work instead of routing to adjacent lanes.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public sources used here are strongest for U.S. institutional cash securities under T+1, while other jurisdictions, asset classes, and market infrastructures can change timing, SSI, and affirmation rules materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of jurisdiction, asset class, custodian model, and governing market practice before issuing final readiness guidance outside the documented baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific delegation for setup maintenance and downstream release authority
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define the control perimeter, but tenants decide whether middle office may update SSIs, publish readiness, or only recommend next actions before settlements or servicing teams act.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve the tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only whenever setup-change or release authority is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to tenant and jurisdiction retrieval at runtime.
The research output constrains the lane to middle-office prerequisite and readiness control rather than generic operations, KYC onboarding, or settlement approval ownership.
